steps.title: Calculate the sample proportion
steps.content: In Scott's sample of 20 employees, 16 are enrolled in exactly three courses. The sample proportion is $$\frac{16}{20} = \frac{4}{5}$$.

steps.title: Scale to the population
steps.content: To estimate the total number in the entire population, multiply the sample proportion by the total number of employees: $$\frac{4}{5} \times 400 = 320$$. That matches choice B — B is correct.
